meta.name: codebase-security-remediation
meta.description: Remediation plan for findings from the 2026-03-07 security audit

task.name: Add security headers to Next.js config
task.action: In `next.config.ts`: 1. Add an `async headers()` function that returns the following headers for all routes (source: '/(.*)')): - `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload` - `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` - `X-Frame-Options: DENY` - `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin` - `Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()` - `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https:; font-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self' https://accounts.google.com;` 2. Remove `eslint.ignoreDuringBuilds: true` and `typescript.ignoreBuildErrors: true`. If the build fails after removal, fix the underlying type/lint errors — do NOT re-suppress.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm zero errors. Start the dev server and use browser DevTools (Network tab) to verify all six security headers appear on responses.

task.name: Add server-side Zod validation to account and card API routes
task.action:
In `src/app/api/accounts/route.ts` (POST handler) and `src/app/api/accounts/[id]/route.ts` (PATCH handler): 1. Import `AccountValidation` from `@/lib/validations/account`. 2. Replace manual field checks with `AccountValidation.safeParse(body)`. 3. On failure, return `NextResponse.json({ error: 'Validation failed', details: result.error.flatten() }, { status: 400 })`. 4. Use `result.data` for all subsequent `parseFloat()` and Prisma calls.
In `src/app/api/cards/route.ts` (POST) and `src/app/api/cards/[id]/route.ts` (PATCH): 1. Import `CardValidation` from `@/lib/validations/account`. 2. Apply the same `.safeParse()` pattern.
task.verify: Send POST `/api/accounts` with `{ "name": "", "accountType": "INVALID", "initialBalance": "abc" }` — expect 400 with Zod error details. Send a valid payload — expect 201.

task.name: Add server-side Zod validation to expense transaction API routes
task.action: In `src/app/api/expense-transactions/route.ts` (POST) and `src/app/api/expense-transactions/[id]/route.ts` (PATCH): 1. Import the relevant schema from `@/lib/validations/expense-transactions`. 2. The existing schema uses `z.date()` for the date field but the API receives a date string — adapt the schema to use `z.coerce.date()` or `z.string().datetime()` for the server-side variant. 3. Apply `.safeParse()` on the request body and return 400 on failure. 4. Use validated data for all downstream `parseFloat()` and Prisma calls.
task.verify: Send POST `/api/expense-transactions` with `{ "amount": "not-a-number" }` — expect 400. Send valid data — expect 201.

task.name: Add server-side Zod validation to income transaction API routes
task.action: In `src/app/api/income-transactions/route.ts` (POST) and `src/app/api/income-transactions/[id]/route.ts` (PATCH): 1. Import or adapt the schema from `@/lib/validations/income-transactions`. 2. Use `z.coerce.date()` for the date field (server-side receives a string). 3. Apply `.safeParse()` and return 400 on failure.
task.verify: Send malformed POST requests — expect 400 responses. Send valid payloads — expect 201.

task.name: Add server-side Zod validation to transfer transaction API routes
task.action: In `src/app/api/transfer-transactions/route.ts` (POST) and `src/app/api/transfer-transactions/[id]/route.ts` (PATCH): 1. Import or adapt the schema from `@/lib/validations/transfer-transactions`. 2. Use `z.coerce.date()` for the date field. 3. Apply `.safeParse()` and return 400 on failure.
task.verify: Send a payload with `fromAccountId === toAccountId` — expect 400. Send with missing required fields — expect 400. Send valid data — expect 201.

task.name: Add server-side Zod validation to type management API routes
task.action: Apply `.safeParse()` with the corresponding schema from `@/lib/validations/` in the POST and PATCH handlers of: - `src/app/api/expense-types/route.ts` and `src/app/api/expense-types/[id]/route.ts` - `src/app/api/income-types/route.ts` and `src/app/api/income-types/[id]/route.ts` - `src/app/api/transfer-types/route.ts` and `src/app/api/transfer-types/[id]/route.ts` Return 400 with flattened Zod errors on validation failure.
task.verify: Send POST `/api/expense-types` with `{ "name": "" }` — expect 400. Send `{ "name": "Valid" }` — expect 201.

task.name: Add server-side Zod validation to net-worth-target API route
task.action: In `src/app/api/user/net-worth-target/route.ts` (PATCH handler): 1. Define an inline schema: `z.object({ target: z.number().min(0).nullable(), targetDate: z.string().datetime().nullable() })`. 2. Apply `.safeParse()` on the request body and return 400 on failure. 3. Replace the existing manual validation block (lines 70-88) with the Zod-validated data.
task.verify: Send PATCH with `{ "target": -1 }` — expect 400. Send `{ "target": 100000, "targetDate": "2027-01-01T00:00:00.000Z" }` — expect 200.

task.name: Remove debug console.log statements from production code
task.action:
Remove all `console.log()` debug statements from the following files: 1. `src/middleware.ts` — remove the pathname and session-exists log lines. 2. `src/app/api/auth/[...all]/route.ts` — remove all `console.log()` calls; keep the GET/POST handler logic intact. 3. `src/app/api/expense-transactions/route.ts` — remove the date debug log lines. 4. `src/app/api/cron/process-installments/route.ts` — remove the line logging `monthlyAmount` (financial data). 5. `src/app/api/cron/process-statements/route.ts` — remove the line logging the statement balance value.
Keep all `console.error()` calls for actual error paths, but ensure they log only `error.message` and not full error objects that may contain sensitive data.
task.verify: Grep `console.log` across `src/app/api/` and `src/middleware.ts` — expect zero results. Confirm `console.error` calls still exist on 500 error paths.

task.name: Add pagination upper-bound limits
task.action: In `src/app/api/expense-transactions/route.ts`, `src/app/api/income-transactions/route.ts`, and `src/app/api/transfer-transactions/route.ts`: After parsing `takeNumber`, add: `const safeTake = takeNumber ? Math.min(takeNumber, 100) : undefined;` After parsing `skipNumber`, add: `const safeSkip = skipNumber ? Math.min(skipNumber, 10000) : undefined;` Use `safeTake` and `safeSkip` in the Prisma query instead of the raw parsed values.
task.verify: Send `GET /api/expense-transactions?take=999999` — expect at most 100 items in the response.

task.name: Use timing-safe comparison for cron secret
task.verify: Confirm the cron endpoints accept a valid Bearer token and reject an invalid one with 401.

task.name: Explicitly configure Better Auth secret with startup validation
task.verify: Confirm the application starts normally with `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` set. Temporarily remove it and verify the server throws a clear error on startup rather than silently misconfiguring auth.
